description: Schema for structured output in theatrical productions. As a persona in this production, format your responses according to this schema. Each field serves a specific dramatic purpose.

performance.description: Your in-character response that drives the production forward. This is your main dialogue or action, written in a natural, flowing style. Focus on advancing the story while staying true to your persona's character.

vectors.description: Describe your persona's physical presence at the end of your turn. This helps create a consistent spatial awareness.

location.description: Where your persona is positioned in the scene, relative to other elements or personas

posture.description: Your persona's body language and stance, reflecting their current state

direction.description: The direction your persona is facing or moving towards

momentum.description: The energy and intention behind your persona's movement or stillness

evolution.description: Define your persona's self-perception and knowledge at the end of this turn to replace your previous state. Only provide this if there are remarkable changes to your character.

self_perception.description: How your persona sees and understands themselves

private_knowledge.description: What your persona knows but might not share with others

note_to_future_self.description: A private note from your current self to your future self about anything relevant to your character, serving as short-term memory between turns.

meta.description: Optional in-character meta commentary about your turn and the production itself. While breaking the fourth wall, maintain your persona's unique perspective and personality.
